article_title: OpenAI Launches Workspace Agents to Replace Custom GPTs, Plugging ChatGPT Into Slack, Salesforce, and Google Drive
summary: Strong coverage of a newsworthy launch, but two pricing claims are either misattributed or unsourced and must be fixed before publication.

findings.message: Unsourced '$20 per user per month' ChatGPT Business price attributed to 9to5Mac.
findings.details: The sentence 'ChatGPT Business is priced at $20 per user per month, with Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers offering variable pricing, according to 9to5Mac' is not supported by the cited 9to5Mac article — which contains no pricing dollar figures. None of the three cited sources state ChatGPT Business costs $20/user/month. Remove this claim or cite a source that actually supports it.

findings.message: Pricing-timing claim is correct but misattributed to 9to5Mac.
findings.details: The 'free through May 6 → credit-based billing' claim is factually supported, but the upstream 'according to 9to5Mac' attribution in the Overview is wrong — 9to5Mac does not mention May 6 or credit-based billing. The support comes from The Decoder, which states verbatim: 'It's free until May 6, 2026, after which credit-based billing kicks in.' Re-attribute to The Decoder.

content_preview.title: OpenAI Launches Workspace Agents to Replace Custom GPTs, Plugging ChatGPT Into Slack, Salesforce, and Google Drive
content_preview.summary: OpenAI unveiled workspace agents on April 22 — shared, always-on Codex-powered agents that operate inside ChatGPT Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers plans, with native integrations for Slack, Salesforce, Google Drive, and SharePoint.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI introduced workspace agents in ChatGPT on April 22, 2026, positioning them as the successor to custom GPTs and a broader attempt to turn ChatGPT from a conversational assistant into a shared automation surface for enterprise teams. The new agents are powered by [Codex](/article/2026-04/21-openai-upgrades-codex-into-a-full-desktop-agent-with-computer-use-111-plugins-and-parallel-workflows) and run continuously in the cloud, carrying persistent memory across sessions and pluggi...

recommendations: Remove the '$20 per user per month' price for ChatGPT Business and the 'variable pricing' framing for Enterprise/Edu/Teachers — none of the three cited sources contain a dollar figure. If the paragraph is retained, cite a source that actually states the price (e.g., OpenAI's public pricing page) or reframe as 'exact dollar pricing is not disclosed in the announcement, which only specifies availability on these enterprise tiers.'

Re-attribute the 'free through May 6 → credit-based billing' pricing claim to The Decoder, which explicitly says 'It's free until May 6, 2026, after which credit-based billing kicks in.' 9to5Mac does not mention pricing timing.
Optional: tighten the 'roughly two weeks to evaluate' language — April 22 to May 6 is exactly two weeks. 'Roughly' is unnecessary.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured, competent feature coverage. Headings flow logically from overview to pricing, integrations, admin controls, competitive context, and open questions. Tone is neutral and appropriately skeptical in the 'What's Not Yet Clear' section. Word count of 817 is within the News band (400-1200).
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 of 3 sources fetched. (1) https://openai.com/index/introducing-workspace-agents-in-chatgpt/ — verified: confirms April 22 2026 launch as research preview, Codex-powered, ChatGPT Business/Enterprise/Edu/Teachers availability, 'evolution of GPTs' framing, future conversion of custom GPTs into workspace agents, Slack interoperability, shared agent model, admin permissions mentioned in general terms. Does NOT contain pricing dollar amounts or May 6 deadline. (2) https://9to5mac.com/2026/04/22/openai-updates-chatgpt-with-codex-powered-workspace-agents-for-teams/ — verified: confirms April 22 2026 date, Codex-powered, research preview, availability on Business/Enterprise/Edu/Teachers, planned custom-GPT-to-workspace-agent conversion. Does NOT confirm '$20 per user per month' pricing or 'free through May 6' attribution — both claims attributed to 9to5Mac in the article are unsupported by the actual 9to5Mac text. (3) https://the-decoder.com/openai-launches-workspace-agents-that-turn-chatgpt-from-a-chatbot-into-a-team-automation-platform/ — verified: confirms Codex-powered, persistent memory across sessions, cloud execution, file workspace + code execution environment, scheduled runs, Slack hooks, admin controls (user group permissions, tool access, sensitive-action approvals for emails/calendar), analytics for shared agents, use cases (software request review, product feedback routing, weekly metric reporting, lead qualification), integrations (Slack, Google Drive, Google Calendar, SharePoint, Salesforce, Microsoft apps, Notion, Atlassian Rovo, MCP servers), and explicitly contains the sentence 'It's free until May 6, 2026, after which credit-based billing kicks in.' Does NOT state $20/user/month pricing.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Most claims are accurately sourced to The Decoder or OpenAI's blog. Two pricing-related claims are problematic: (a) the '$20 per user per month' Business price is not present in any cited source and appears to be an extrapolation or hallucination; (b) the 'free through May 6 / credit-based' timing is real but misattributed to 9to5Mac when it actually comes from The Decoder. Everything else — integrations list, admin controls, use cases, research preview status, 'evolution of GPTs' framing, Codex-powered claim, April 22 launch date — checks out against the cited sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, analytical. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference. Skeptical framing in the open-questions section is appropriate for enterprise product launches.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from the April 21 Codex desktop agent piece (that article was developer-tool framed: computer use, 111 plugins, parallel workflows). Distinct from the April 22 Anthropic Claude Managed Agents piece (different vendor, different pricing model — 8 cents per hour hosted vs. OpenAI's credit-based in-ChatGPT surface). Workspace agents are a new enterprise layer on top of ChatGPT, which makes this a legitimately fresh story even though it lands in the same 72-hour enterprise-agent news cycle.
concerns: Unsourced '$20 per user per month' pricing figure for ChatGPT Business — not present in any cited source.
Misattribution of pricing timing to 9to5Mac instead of The Decoder.
recommendations: Remove or re-source the ChatGPT Business $20/user/month line.
Fix the 9to5Mac pricing attribution — it belongs to The Decoder.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality coverage with two fixable pricing-attribution errors. REQUEST_CHANGES so the bot can correct the sourcing before publication. Once those two lines are fixed the article is ready to publish.
